stem: A public health study surveys 15 residents surveyed and observes 6 positive outcomes out of 15 (40.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 25.0%) and Group B (7 observations, rate = 57.1%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 37% and 57% of the population. Estimate the true vaccination coverage and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.4. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.4368 (range [0.4082, 0.4654]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.1982, 0.6425]. Point estimate: 0.4368.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.47: 0.47×0.285 + 0.53×0.5714 = 0.4368.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
